Object.description: The root class of the Smalltalk class hierarchy from which all other classes inherit, providing fundamental behavior for identity, equality, copying, printing, and message handling.

printString.description: Returns a string representation of the receiver for display purposes.

class.description: Returns the class of the receiver object.

copy.description: Returns a shallow copy of the receiver.

respondsTo.description: Returns true if the receiver can respond to the specified message selector.

yourself.description: Returns the receiver itself, useful for cascaded message sends.

isNil.description: Returns true if the receiver is nil (the undefined object).

Collection.description: Abstract superclass for all collection types providing common enumeration, testing, conversion, and element access protocols shared by arrays, sets, dictionaries, and other containers.

do:.description: Evaluates the block for each element in the collection for side effects.

collect:.description: Returns a new collection of results from evaluating the block for each element.

select:.description: Returns a new collection containing only elements for which the block returns true.

reject:.description: Returns a new collection excluding elements for which the block returns true.

detect:.description: Returns the first element for which the block returns true, raising an error if none found.

inject:into:.description: Reduces the collection by accumulating through the block starting from the initial value.

size.description: Returns the number of elements in the collection.

includes:.description: Returns true if the collection contains the specified element.

OrderedCollection.description: A dynamically-sized ordered collection that maintains insertion order and supports adding, removing, and accessing elements by integer index, similar to dynamic arrays in other languages.

add:.description: Appends an object to the end of the collection and returns it.

addFirst:.description: Inserts an object at the beginning of the collection.

remove:.description: Removes the first occurrence of the specified object from the collection.

at:.description: Returns the element at the specified one-based index position.

at:put:.signature: anOrderedCollection at: index put: anObject -> Object
at:put:.description: Replaces the element at the specified index with the given object.

removeAt:.description: Removes and returns the element at the specified index.

Dictionary.description: A hash-based collection that maps unique keys to values using associations, providing fast key-based lookup, insertion, and removal with enumeration over key-value pairs.

at:put:.signature: aDictionary at: key put: value -> value
at:put:.description: Associates the given key with the value, replacing any existing association for that key.

at:.description: Returns the value associated with the key, raising an error if the key is not found.

at:ifAbsent:.signature: aDictionary at: key ifAbsent: aBlock -> value
at:ifAbsent:.description: Returns the value for the key, or evaluates the block if the key is not present.

keys.description: Returns a set of all keys in the dictionary.

values.description: Returns a collection of all values in the dictionary.

keysAndValuesDo:.description: Evaluates the two-argument block for each key-value pair in the dictionary.

includesKey:.description: Returns true if the dictionary contains the specified key.

String.description: An immutable sequence of characters representing textual data, supporting pattern matching, substring operations, conversion, and comparison through the rich Smalltalk string protocol.

size.description: Returns the number of characters in the string.

copyFrom:to:.signature: aString copyFrom: start to: end -> String
copyFrom:to:.description: Returns a substring from the start index to the end index inclusive.

asUppercase.description: Returns a new string with all characters converted to uppercase.

asLowercase.description: Returns a new string with all characters converted to lowercase.

asInteger.description: Parses the string as an integer value.

includesSubstring:.description: Returns true if the string contains the specified substring.

Number.description: Abstract superclass for all numeric types including Integer, Float, and Fraction, providing arithmetic operations, comparisons, rounding, mathematical functions, and iteration support.

abs.description: Returns the absolute value of the receiver.

max:.description: Returns the larger of the receiver and the argument.

min:.description: Returns the smaller of the receiver and the argument.

to:do:.description: Iterates from start to end, evaluating the block with each integer value.

to:by:do:.signature: start to: end by: step do: [:i | ...]
to:by:do:.description: Iterates from start to end with the specified step increment.

rounded.description: Returns the integer nearest to the receiver.

Block.description: A first-class closure that captures its lexical environment and can be stored, passed as an argument, and evaluated on demand, forming the basis of all Smalltalk control structures.

value.description: Evaluates a zero-argument block and returns the result.

value:.description: Evaluates a one-argument block with the given argument.

value:value:.signature: aBlock value: arg1 value: arg2 -> Object
value:value:.description: Evaluates a two-argument block with the given arguments.

whileTrue:.description: Repeatedly evaluates the body block while the condition block returns true.

whileFalse:.description: Repeatedly evaluates the body block while the condition block returns false.

on:do:.description: Evaluates the block and handles the specified exception class with the handler block.

Stream.signature: ReadStream on: collection | WriteStream on: collection
Stream.description: Provides sequential read and write access to collections through a positioned cursor, supporting character-by-character or element-by-element traversal and accumulation of data.

next.description: Returns the next element from the stream and advances the position.

next:.description: Returns the next count elements from the stream as a collection.

nextPut:.description: Writes an object to the stream at the current position.

nextPutAll:.description: Writes all elements of the collection to the stream.

atEnd.description: Returns true if the stream position is at or past the end.

contents.description: Returns all elements written to the stream so far as a collection.

reset.description: Resets the stream position to the beginning.

BOOL.description: Boolean data type. Can be TRUE or FALSE.

TIME.description: Duration type. Represents time intervals like T#5s or T#100ms.

ARRAY.description: Fixed-size array of elements of the same type.

FUNCTION_BLOCK.description: A reusable program organization unit with persistent data. Can be instantiated multiple times.

FUNCTION.description: A program organization unit that returns a value. Has no persistent data.

PROGRAM.description: The top-level program organization unit. Contains the main execution logic.

VAR.description: Variable declaration block for local variables.

VAR_INPUT.description: Declares input parameters for function blocks.

VAR_OUTPUT.description: Declares output parameters for function blocks.

VAR_GLOBAL.description: Declares global variables accessible across programs.

IF.signature: IF condition THEN ... ELSIF ... ELSE ... END_IF

CASE.signature: CASE expression OF value: ... ELSE ... END_CASE
CASE.description: Multi-branch selection construct based on the value of an expression.

FOR.signature: FOR var := start TO end BY step DO ... END_FOR
FOR.description: Iterates with a counter from start to end.

WHILE.description: Repeats while the condition is true.

REPEAT.description: Repeats until the condition becomes true.

TON.description: On-delay timer. Output is set after a specified time delay.

TOF.description: Off-delay timer. Output is reset after a specified time delay.

CTU.description: Up counter. Counts up each time the CU input transitions from FALSE to TRUE.

R_TRIG.description: Rising edge detector. Output is TRUE for one scan cycle when input transitions FALSE to TRUE.

F_TRIG.description: Falling edge detector. Output is TRUE for one scan cycle when input transitions TRUE to FALSE.

MAX.description: Returns the maximum of two values.

MIN.description: Returns the minimum of two values.

LIMIT.description: Limits a value to a range [MN, MX].

SEL.signature: SEL(G : BOOL, IN0, IN1 : ANY) : ANY
SEL.description: Binary selector. Returns IN0 if G=FALSE, IN1 if G=TRUE.

MUX.signature: MUX(K : ANY_INT, IN0, IN1, ... : ANY) : ANY
MUX.description: Multiplexer. Selects one of N inputs based on K.

MOVE.description: Assigns the input value to the output.

LEN.description: Returns the length of a string.

FIND.description: Finds the position of the first occurrence of IN2 in IN1.

MID.signature: MID(IN : STRING, L : INT, P : INT) : STRING
MID.description: Returns L characters starting at position P.
